acceptance_checks.name: Gotcha registry consulted
acceptance_checks.notes: No gotcha registry file found at artifacts/harness/gotcha-registry*. Gotchas referenced from CLAUDE.md and carry-forwards: G070 (__pycache__ cleared after src/aho/ touch), G071 (daemon restart — no imports changed), G078 (schema v3), G079 (baseline backstop), G081 (no celebratory framing), G082 (canonical paths), G083 (no except Exception in new code). All honored.

findings: Baseline at 14, not 13. Two new failures from 0.2.13 close reconciliation: test_structural_gates_emits_checks and test_gate_returns_gate_result_dict. These were predicted to resolve at 0.2.14 open but did not. They remain in baseline as known failures. One baseline failure fixed: test_healthy_unit_passes (DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS now available).

The emit_workstream_complete side-effect bug was confirmed live: calling emit_workstream_start('W0') on the 0.2.13 closed checkpoint corrupted W1, W3-W6 sibling states to in_progress. Root cause unclear — the code path in emit_workstream_start only modifies the named workstream's dict entry. Possible concurrent write or MANIFEST daemon interaction. The fix adds checkpoint updating to emit_workstream_complete (which previously had none) and the test verifies sibling preservation. The root corruption mechanism in emit_workstream_start needs further investigation in W1 if it recurs.

Existing test suite (test_workstream_events.py) does NOT mock find_project_root, causing real checkpoint corruption when tests run. The new test_emit_sibling_preservation.py properly mocks it. The existing tests should be patched to use isolated project roots — deferred, not in W0 scope.

Gotcha registry file not found at expected location (artifacts/harness/gotcha-registry*). Gotchas referenced inline from CLAUDE.md, design docs, and carry-forwards. Registry may exist under a different name or be embedded in another artifact.
CLAUDE.md updated to 0.2.14. GEMINI.md NOT updated (out of W0 scope — Gemini's instructions for audit session).

No workstream_start event was logged to the event log because AHO_ITERATION was null at the time of the call (pre-version-bump). The checkpoint was updated manually. Future workstream starts should happen after version bumps.
